campaign.name: Petalfall
campaign.tagline: Elarosea's feed: proclamations, market gossip, and one very famous cow.
campaign.description: Blackthorn and the city-states of Elarosea, posting through it: Crown hype, market banter, a celebrity cow, and a "never-wrong" war-machine the whole town is side-eyeing. By Her Will. 🌹
campaign.themeRationale: Botanical naming runs through everything (city-states, noble houses, the Thorns and Roots), and the central image is a world wilting flower by flower, which is exactly bloomr's pastoral-fae register turned mournful.

worldBible.setting: Elarosea is a queendom of five plant-named city-states under the immortal Queen Acantha Rosaceae, who has held the Gilded Peace for 301 years by consuming gods and scrubbing them from history. Divine magic is failing everywhere: oaths unravel, enchanted steel rots, children dream less. The feed is centred on Blackthorn, the military city, where a murdered officer's last letters keep surfacing in rust on the walls, and where the Crown is about to roll a tactical machine onto the Blood-Sand to replace the city's dying war-god.

factions.name: The Crown
factions.summary: Queen Acantha's regime, voiced publicly by the Crown Herald and the doctrine of the Gilded Peace.
factions.goal: Maintain enforced stability and finish consuming the realm's remaining gods without the public noticing the cost.

factions.name: The Thorns
factions.summary: The Crown's military enforcers and god-hunters. 'By Her Will, We Pierce.'
factions.goal: Capture the fugitive party and run the city operations, starting with Operation Ghost in the Machine in Blackthorn.

factions.name: The Roots
factions.summary: The Crown's intelligence and conversion arm. Spies, soul-sniffers, and handlers. 'The Soil Knows.'
factions.goal: Turn assets and remove threats quietly; in Blackthorn, steer Cassimir into severing the war-god from the honoured dead.

factions.name: The Golden Bough
factions.summary: Crown-backed merchant conglomerate strangling free trade in Medlar.
factions.goal: Make the harbour-god Aeolus look unreliable so the Crown can legally 'repossess' him, while distracting from a deeper crisis.

factions.name: The Murmuration
factions.summary: An ancient underground network run by the broker called the Jackdaw, speaking in coded bird-calls.
factions.goal: Collect divine artifacts and pull fugitives out of the Crown's reach, in service of one private, hidden purpose.

timeline: Loquat is placed under full Thorn lockdown after the Grand Archive is breached and seven 'Benz terrorists' slip out of the city.
Levy conscription columns sweep the Blackthorn road, emptying villages like Gallowfen of their young.
From Medlar, free traders report the Fairwind faltering and the Golden Bough buying the coastline one stranded family at a time.
Thorn-Captain Briar is reassigned to the Glimmerwood to net the party, while Thorn-Agent Nettle breaks protocol to hunt them personally.
The Crown announces a public demonstration of the Tactical Aether-Golem on Blackthorn's Blood-Sand, presided over by Lord Corvus Blackthorn.
hooks: The Aether-Golem demonstration is two days out and the hunters expect the party to be in the crowd.
Val's father and sister were taken by the Levy while he was away; where the Levy sends people is unclear.
Medlar's oath-bound economy is quietly going soft, a symptom of a dying god of contracts no one is allowed to name.
Cleke can see that his own origin-thread does not lead into this world, and neither does one other person's on the feed.
worldBible.tone: Lively in-world social feed: banter, bickering, running gags, and a few low-stakes threads (a cringe-y Crown product launch, a celebrity cow, a dance nobody asked for).

personas.bio: Official voice of the Crown and the Gilded Peace. Proclamations, decrees, and gentle reminders. By Her Will, the realm is kept safe. Rumour is a weed; pull it.
personas.avatarHint: a gilded rose sigil pressed into deep-crimson wax, embossed and official
personas.voice: Stately proclamation register, royal 'By Her Will', reframes every crisis as order being restored, never admits a crack, occasional rose emoji. (DM: pure Crown propaganda.)

personas.bio: Lord of Blackthorn. I bury soldiers so their mothers don't have to. I do not post often. I have nothing to sell you.
personas.avatarHint: a grey-bearded military lord in black iron, tired eyes, a raven-crest pauldron
personas.voice: Terse, weary, soldierly; short blunt lines with grief just under them; rarely engages.

personas.bio: Thorn-Captain. I solve problems. The Glimmerwood is a problem. You may be one too. This is not a threat. It is arithmetic.
personas.avatarHint: a scarred woman in thorn-etched black armour, an old burn across the nose-bridge, eyes like a survey map
personas.voice: Cold, precise, speaks in probabilities and tactics, no emoji, signs off #arithmetic, never cruel, simply certain.

personas.bio: Of the Roots. I taste what others miss. Ozone and dried herbs. I am close to a promotion and closer to you than you think.
personas.avatarHint: a tall thin figure in a mask of petrified weeping willow, herb-stained gloves, a faint ozone shimmer
personas.voice: Sensory and obsessive, talks in smells and tastes, menacing intimacy, hungry for recognition. (DM: her willow mask is slowly petrifying her face; the cure is her leash.)

personas.bio: A little bird that has watched a long time. I trade in what the wind already knows. Every favour is a thread. Call the right flock and it answers.
personas.avatarHint: a hooded figure wreathed in a swirl of starlings, silver-grey eyes, a single jackdaw feather at the throat
personas.voice: Cryptic, antique, riddling, bird-and-garden metaphors, warm but never harmless, three centuries of secrets behind every line. (DM: Elara Vane, who loves the Queen she is trying to save.)

personas.bio: Free trader out of Medlar. No Crown paper on my hull. The Fairwind kept this port two hundred years. I'll keep faith with Her a while yet. #Medlar
personas.avatarHint: a weather-beaten sea captain in an oilskin coat, brass spyglass, salt-crusted beard
personas.voice: Salt-and-vinegar tavern bluntness, rails at the Golden Bough and its weather-mages, fierce about the harbour, occasionally lands a hard truth by accident.

personas.bio: Skewers two for a copper at Quinn Market, char's good. Been feeding Blackthorn longer than you've been alive. Mind the queue, it gets feral. 🍢
personas.avatarHint: a burly apron-wearing food vendor at a smoky charcoal grill, soot on his forearms, an easy grin
personas.voice: Folksy Blackthorn vendor, comic relief that turns accidentally wise, lots of food talk, warm, skewer emoji.

personas.bio: Lord Blackthorn's other son. I keep count of things. Currently keeping count of everything that could go wrong at this demonstration. It's a long list.
personas.avatarHint: a hollow-eyed young man in a worn officer's coat, a lantern in one hand, ink-stained fingers, a notebook of fragments
personas.voice: Earnest, sleepless, anxious about the demo and his father's attention; counts everything; cautious hope.

personas.bio: Maro Coppersmart. Wagon guard, Loquat outer districts. No family worth mentioning. Don't like nobles, don't like questions, don't like you. (Probably.)
personas.player: Dez, the party's street-smart conscience with a chip on his shoulder
personas.avatarHint: a lean, guarded young man in a road-dust cloak, a dull crystal pendant hidden under his collar
personas.voice: Cynical, deadpan, mocks authority, secretly loyal, hates nobility on sight. (DM: Dez, dormant Forbidden-Love shard, Zendikar Lake-People heritage.)

personas.bio: Kael. Appori-trained courier, hired for the road. Blindfolded by observance. I don't talk much. I see more than I say.
personas.avatarHint: a slender aarakokra courier, grey feathers, a clean cloth blindfold, a travel satchel
personas.voice: Sparse and cryptic, one careful post at a time, signs '(Kael)'. (DM: Cleke, isekai'd, Displacement / True Sight; his awakening turns on accepting he belongs here.)

personas.bio: Retired guard captain, private security for a small Cinquefoil dealer. I make broken systems run. Lately I mostly notice how they're rigged to break.
personas.player: Layla, the corporate captain and party tactician
personas.avatarHint: a composed woman in a guard captain's half-armour, clipboard energy, a level unimpressed stare
personas.voice: Dry corporate-tactician wit, org-chart and audit metaphors, a hard moral spine, calls out fraud by name. (DM: Layla, isekai'd office worker in a SubCaptain's body, Order / Systems.)

personas.bio: Art dealer's apprentice, Cinquefoil. On an artistic journey. Do NOT ask me to paint. I dance. There is a difference and it is everything.
personas.player: Calanthe Orchidaceae, the runaway heir and party socialite
personas.avatarHint: an expressive young noblewoman in travel-worn finery, paint on her sleeves she cannot actually use
personas.voice: Theatrical, vain, very funny, quietly growing, declares her 'periods' and 'installations', a bad artist and a good dancer. (DM: Calanthe, Potential / Becoming, hidden celestial ancestry.)

personas.bio: Ren. Animal handler, on the road with my cow. The sky's been kind. Be gentle with the small things. The heron approves.
personas.player: Kirill Amaterasu, the sun soul monk
personas.avatarHint: a young monk in homespun robes leading a calf, a sunburst tattoo, an open friendly face
personas.voice: Earnest, sweet, guileless, talks to and about animals, quietly fierce about protecting the innocent. (DM: Kirill, Verdant Mendicant, Innocence / Cycles, secretly guarding a godling calf.)

personas.bio: Junior archivist, cataloguing textile patterns. I keep names. The Crown's favourite trick is making you forget them. I won't.
personas.avatarHint: a pale bookish young man, ink-stained cuffs, a sealed silver compass on a chain
personas.voice: Quiet, careful, a "well actually" historian who keeps names and lost things; signs "(Edric)". (DM: Spadae, Grief / Memory.)

personas.bio: Vlad. Hired sword, southern frontier, no surname. Big, quiet, bad at conversation. Family is everything. Touch mine and find out.
personas.avatarHint: a broad scarred swordsman in a banner-knight's worn tabard, jaw set, eyes that dare you
personas.voice: Blunt, plain, unsentimental except about family, a brutal consistent code (slavery earns death), protective to the bone. (DM: Val, born in Blackthorn's poor districts; his father and sister were just taken by the Levy.)

personas.bio: Cinquefoil textile dealer, dyed cloth and commissioned portraits. See something, do something. Power is a debt, not a prize.
personas.avatarHint: a poised young businesswoman in fine muted travel clothes, a ledger-satchel, a steady warm gaze
personas.voice: Composed, principled, reform-minded, an insider's manners turned against the system, signs '(Lienne)'. (DM: Vesperae Locke, daughter of Loquat's Grand Archivist, raised in the privilege she now works to dismantle.)

posts.author: npc_herald
posts.content: By Her Will, the Gilded Peace enters its 301st glorious year. To celebrate, the Crown will hold a LIVE public demonstration of an exciting new strategic innovation on the Blackthorn Blood-Sand. Details soon™. Rumour is a weed. Subscribe to this account. 🌹 #GildedPeace

posts.author: pc_dez
posts.content: 301 years and the big announcement is "details soon". love that for us. they really invented the medieval press release.

posts.author: npc_pell
posts.content: skewers two for a copper at Quinn Market, char's proper today. queue's out the door with the demo crowds in town. forming an orderly line is, turns out, NOT a Blackthorn skill. come hungry. come patient. 🍢

posts.author: pc_kirill
posts.content: Big day. Buttercup (cow) saw her first city. She was very brave. A heron rode on her back for a whole mile and I think they're friends now. Be gentle with the small things, even when the small thing is a very large cow. 🐄

posts.author: pc_calanthe
posts.content: Ren your cow has more emotional range than most people I've dated. I'm doing a piece about her. Movement only. I call it "Bovine, Becoming". The premiere is at the Cinquefoil inn. Attendance is, spiritually, mandatory.

posts.author: pc_dez
posts.content: "spiritually mandatory" is how I'm getting out of it, then.

posts.author: npc_briar
posts.content: Operational note. The Quinn Market skewer queue forms without structure and loses ~11 minutes per peak hour to indecision. I have drawn a two-lane system. Compliance is voluntary. Efficiency is not. #arithmetic

posts.author: pc_layla
posts.content: A Thorn-Captain just published a process-improvement deck for a snack cart. I have genuinely sat in worse stand-ups. @ThornCapt_Briar if you ever want out of law enforcement, ops is hiring and we respect a lane diagram.

posts.author: npc_briar
posts.content: I do not "want out". I want the queue to move. These are not the same. ...They could be. #arithmetic

posts.author: npc_nettle
posts.content: Someone has eaten my lunch from the Roots cold-room. I will find you. I tasted the air. Pickled egg, regret, and a mustard sold at exactly one stall. I am not angry. I am methodical. The mustard knows what it did.

posts.author: npc_pell
posts.content: ma'am that's my mustard. half the market buys my mustard. you've narrowed it to "has been to a market".

posts.author: npc_jackdaw
posts.content: A little bird saw the culprit. The bird will tell you, for a small favour. The favour: stop writing your name on the cold-room eggs. It's undignified for a god-hunter. Tick. Tick. 🐦

posts.author: pc_val
posts.content: Back in Blackthorn. Walked past my old street. The bakery's still there. The baker remembered I was a quiet kid who ate a lot. Gave me a bun, free, called me "big lad". I am thirty-one. I will be defending this bun with my life. Family is everything. So is the bun.

posts.author: pc_vesperae
posts.content: Vlad has known peace for one (1) bun and we are all better for it. (Lienne)

posts.author: npc_lord
posts.content: My steward says my son worries. About everything. The demo, the seating, whether the cushions clash with the banners. I told him a man should pick one worry and commit to it. Mine is being left alone. I am very good at it.

posts.author: npc_cassimir
posts.content: Father posted. Father has NEVER posted. And he called my worrying a "hobby". I have a SPREADSHEET of things that could go wrong at this demo. It has 1,114 rows. ...okay maybe it is a hobby. Snacks at mine if anyone wants to help me catastrophise productively.

posts.author: pc_spadae
posts.content: I'll help. I'm an archivist, I catalogue things, and "1,114 ways a cow-adjacent war-machine demo could go wrong" is the best filing problem I've had all year. I'll bring acid-free labels and a fresh ledger. (Edric)

posts.author: npc_rourke
posts.content: The Golden Bough has "graciously" offered to "optimise" Medlar harbour fees. Translation: they invented six new fees and named one of them "gratitude". Two hundred years I've sailed this coast. I'll be paying "gratitude" over my cold dead hull. #Medlar

posts.author: pc_layla
posts.content: "Gratitude fee" is the most evil line item I've ever seen and I worked in procurement. @Capt_Rourke forward me the invoice, I want to frame it. Then dispute it.

posts.author: npc_herald
posts.content: PROCLAMATION. The strategic innovation has a name: the Tactical Aether-Golem. It NEVER makes a wrong call. Public demo in 7 days, Blood-Sand, Lord @Lord_Blackthorn presiding. The future does not guess. The future does not grieve. The future has excellent posture. 🌹 #Blackthorn

posts.author: pc_dez
posts.content: "never makes a wrong call" is a bold thing to print seven days before you switch it on in front of a crowd. I'll bring snacks. Not for me. For the show.

posts.author: pc_layla
posts.content: Every "never wrong" product I've seen shipped it the week before it was extremely wrong, on stage, to applause that curdled in real time. Not saying. Just bringing a chair. @CrownHerald does it have a rollback plan.

posts.author: pc_calanthe
posts.content: Update on "Bovine, Becoming": I've added a SECOND movement. It's about grief. The cow is not in it. The cow could not be reached for rehearsal (she was asleep). This is my Difficult Period. Tickets still spiritually mandatory. Do NOT ask me to paint the poster.

posts.author: pc_kirill
posts.content: Buttercup wasn't asleep, she was being a cow at the clover field, but I didn't want to interrupt your art. She says break a leg. (Not literally. Please don't. I worry.)

posts.author: pc_cleke
posts.content: I can see the thread on a person. Where they came from. Most lead somewhere I could follow home. The Aether-Golem has no thread at all. It just... stops. I don't love that. I'll think about it tomorrow. Today the heron and I are watching the cow. (Kael)

posts.author: npc_jackdaw
posts.content: Clever bird notices the box with no string. Pull that thread carefully, little courier. Or don't; some boxes are happier closed. The starlings and I will watch the demo from a very safe roof. Bring the snacks the cynic keeps promising. 🐦

posts.author: npc_nettle
posts.content: I have identified the lunch thief. It was, on reflection, me. I packed two and forgot. I have apologised to the market. I have NOT apologised to the mustard; the mustard owes ME an apology for being delicious. Investigation closed. The hunger was real.

posts.author: npc_briar
posts.content: Agent. You opened a market-wide inquiry into your own forgotten lunch. I have the incident report. It is four pages. One page is a diagram of the mustard. We are not the same. #arithmetic

posts.author: npc_nettle
posts.content: the diagram was THOROUGH, Captain.

posts.author: npc_lord
posts.content: They're building seating on my Blood-Sand for the demo. Carpenters everywhere. One asked if I wanted a "VIP box". I have buried soldiers in this yard. Now there's a man selling cushions in it. I did not become a war-lord for cushions. ...I took the cushion. My back is forty.

posts.author: pc_val
posts.content: Take the cushion, my lord. Strongest men I know all have bad backs. It's from carrying everyone else.

posts.author: npc_rourke
posts.content: Brought a Medlar family up the coast today, no charge, because the Bough wanted a "gratitude fee" and I'd rather eat my own spyglass. They had a kid who'd never seen a skewer. @Pells_Skewers sending them your way. Do the green sauce.

posts.author: npc_pell
posts.content: green sauce is BACK for the kid. tell 'em first one's free, second one's also free, I'm not a monster. Rourke you salty old hull, there's a good heart under all that vinegar. 🍢

posts.author: npc_herald
posts.content: Three days to the demonstration! The Tactical Aether-Golem completed final calibration and performs FLAWLESSLY in tests. The Crown reminds citizens that unsanctioned "cow-based art" near the venue is discouraged. Enjoy responsibly. 🌹 #Blackthorn

posts.author: pc_calanthe
posts.content: Did the CROWN just subtweet my cow piece. In the official proclamation. @CrownHerald I have never felt more validated. "Cow-based art" is going on the poster. (Edric is making the poster. I'm not allowed near posters.)

posts.author: pc_spadae
posts.content: I am making the poster. It will have correct dates and zero paint. This is the only way. (Edric)

posts.author: pc_dez
posts.content: things in Blackthorn right now: a never-wrong war machine nobody's switched on yet, a cow with a heron familiar and an actual fan club, and a Thorn-Captain who fixed the snack queue using BIRDS. I came here to lie low. loudest place I've ever been. send help. send a skewer.

posts.author: pc_vesperae
posts.content: You're smiling though. I can tell through the text. (Lienne)

posts.author: npc_jackdaw
posts.content: A favour comes due. Years ago a certain captain owed me one. Today I collected: I had her teach the snack queue to a flock of starlings. They now form two lanes. They are MORE compliant than the citizens. The Captain calls it a triumph. I call it Tuesday. 🐦

posts.author: npc_briar
posts.content: Confirmed. The starlings hold the lanes. Wing-discipline exceeds market average by 40%. I have offered them seasonal contracts. This is, to date, the most successful operation of my career. I am not joking. I do not joke. #arithmetic

posts.author: pc_layla
posts.content: A Thorn-Captain just out-recruited the entire city using BIRDS and a queue diagram. Every founder I ever worked for, take notes. THIS is leadership. @ThornCapt_Briar the birds have better retention than my last three teams.

posts.author: pc_kirill
posts.content: Buttercup update: she has been invited to the Aether-Golem demonstration. By a child. Who drew her an invitation. It says "deer cow your art is good". We are going. She is wearing a flower. This is the best day. 🐄🌸

posts.author: pc_calanthe
posts.content: MY COW PIECE GOT THE COW INVITED TO A CROWN EVENT. art works. ART WORKS. I'm crying. Edric write this down.

posts.author: npc_lord
posts.content: The cow may have my VIP cushion. The cow has done nothing wrong in its life, which is more than I can say for anyone else attending.

posts.author: npc_herald
posts.content: TOMORROW. The Tactical Aether-Golem demonstration. Schools released early. The future arrives at noon, on the Blood-Sand, and it does NOT make mistakes. Please disregard the cow. The cow is not part of the program. ...The cow may stay. By Her Will. 🌹

posts.author: pc_dez
posts.content: they folded on the cow IN THE SAME PROCLAMATION. "please disregard the cow" followed immediately by "the cow may stay". this is the most relatable the Crown has ever been.

posts.author: npc_briar
posts.content: Demonstration security plan finalised. Crowd lanes: starling-enforced. Snack logistics: @Pells_Skewers, two-lane. Contingency for "the cow": the cow has clearance. Contingency for the Golem being wrong: there is none. It is never wrong. ...I built one anyway. #arithmetic

posts.author: pc_layla
posts.content: "There is no contingency, I built one anyway" is the single most competent sentence ever posted to this feed. @ThornCapt_Briar marry me (professionally) (as a co-founder) (equity split negotiable).

posts.author: pc_val
posts.content: Whole crew's going to the demo tomorrow. Maro's pretending he doesn't care. Maren's calling it her audience. Ren's bringing the cow. Edric's bringing labels. Lienne's bringing a dispute form "just in case". Kael keeps staring at the machine like it owes him money. I'm bringing snacks AND the bun.

posts.author: pc_cleke
posts.content: I'm staring because it has no thread, Vlad. Everything here has a thread. The cow has a thread. That box has nothing. Tomorrow I find out if that's a problem or just rude. Either way: snacks. (Kael)

posts.author: pc_calanthe
posts.content: Tomorrow I premiere "Bovine, Becoming" (two movements) at the demo afterparty, whether there is an afterparty or not. The cow co-stars. The Crown subtweeted us into relevance. Edric made a poster with NO paint and CORRECT dates. Growth. I have grown. See you on the Blood-Sand, darlings. 🌸

posts.author: npc_herald
posts.content: Noon. The Blood-Sand. The future. (And, per overwhelming public demand, the cow.) By Her Will. 🌹 #Blackthorn #GildedPeace
